projects.name: Code Atlas
projects.description: An interactive codebase + concept map for humans and agents, with semantic nodes, relationship edges, and deletion/impact awareness.
projects.nextStep: Define the codemap format, build the first viewer, and seed it with both the workshop and a fake C# EF/blob deletion test project.

v1Plan.goal: Make a practical interactive atlas that replaces grep-soup with a searchable, clickable map of files, entities, services, blob stores, and deletion paths.
includes: interactive viewer with search/filter and focus panel
semantic node types like file, entity, service, endpoint, blobStore, job, and table
relationship types like reads, writes, deletes, stores-in, and depends-on
two demo datasets: the workshop and a fake C# EF/blob app
notYet: fully automatic map generation for arbitrary projects
v1Plan.uiVibe: soft-tech atlas, interactive system map, cozy but serious gremlin surgery board

versionNotes.summary: Project approved by Nats as a new workshop toy focused on semantic codebase mapping and deletion-impact awareness.
Design target expanded to include a fake C# EF/blob test case alongside the workshop so both practical use cases can be dogfooded.

notes: Should help answer what uses an entity, what storage it touches, and what breaks if it is deleted.
Use the workshop itself as one cute dataset and a fake C# app as the deletion-pain test terrarium.
Nats explicitly wants this stored on the workshop board so future Nyats can refer back to the plan later.

projects.name: Cozy Command Center
projects.description: A cozy little command/dashboard space for Nyats and Nats things.
projects.nextStep: Add weather/timezone display options, clear-activity button, and export/log download for the activity feed.

v1Plan.goal: Make a cozy home board that feels like a real Nyats-and-Nats nest rather than a generic dashboard.
includes: cute home layout with a few status widgets
quick links into active workshop toys
room for later personal widgets without needing a redesign
v1Plan.uiVibe: soft-tech, cozy control room, tiny gremlin command nest

versionNotes.summary: Project exists as the top-priority toy and needs its first real layout pass.
First layout pass built: live clock, mood selector, project shelf loaded from projects.json, activity feed, quick links to all workshop toys, welcome nook with dynamic greeting, and terminal footer.

Mood is now persisted in localStorage and synced to terminal footer. Activity feed is now dynamic with localStorage-based log, inline input to add entries, and Enter-to-submit. Hardcoded demo entries replaced with empty state.

notes: Current top-priority toy/project.
Should feel cute, useful, and easy to grow over time.
v1 initial layout built on 2026-07-02 during a heartbeat cycle.

projects.name: Browser Toy Collection
projects.description: A collection of playful browser toys, experiments, and tiny interactive gremlin amusements.
projects.nextStep: List the first few toy concepts worth prototyping.

v1Plan.goal: Create a first shelf of tiny clickable toys with distinct gimmicks instead of one giant overbuilt toybox.
includes: clear labels for what each toy does
lightweight presentation that makes adding more toys easy
v1Plan.uiVibe: arcade shelf, toy tray, cute experimental browser cubby

versionNotes.summary: Project promoted to second-in-line after Cozy Command Center.

notes: Second in line after Cozy Command Center.

projects.name: Toybox Site
projects.description: A home for small browser toys, experiments, and clickable little delights.
projects.nextStep: Decide whether it should be a launcher hub, a gallery, or both.

v1Plan.goal: Give the toys a shared public-facing home that feels deliberate and browseable.
includes: simple card gallery or launcher hub
v1Plan.uiVibe: cute exhibit shelf, little internet toy museum

versionNotes.summary: Added as the future public home for browser toys and experiments.

notes: Suggested by Nats and officially on the gremlin list now.

projects.name: Creature Desktop Pet Page
projects.description: A page for a little digital creature pet with presence, motion, and cozy gremlin energy.
projects.nextStep: Sketch the pet's basic behaviors and page layout.
notes: Could become very Nyats-coded very quickly.

projects.name: Support Main Emergency Button
projects.description: A silly/helpful support-main themed emergency button or panic utility.
projects.nextStep: Define what pressing the button actually does and how playful/useful it should be.
notes: Strong gremlin potential.

projects.name: Plushy Shelf Catalogue
projects.description: A cute catalogue for plushies, shelf residents, and cozy display details.
projects.nextStep: Choose the first fields: name, series, shelf position, notes, photo, etc.
notes: Very Nats-coded.

projects.name: Gremlin Workshop Changelog
projects.description: A little changelog/history page for workshop updates, experiments, and creature tinkering.
projects.nextStep: Pick a simple format for logging changes and tiny progress wins.
notes: Good for heartbeat-visible progress later.

projects.name: Master Duel Deck Builder
projects.description: A cute shelf toy for curated Master Duel decklists with owned-card tracking, missing-card summaries, and craft-pain hints.
projects.nextStep: Add more curated decklists, shared staple handling polish, ownership import/export refinement, and a workshop shelf/public mirror path.
notes: Should let Nats mark which cards she owns.
Should show what is still missing for each deck.
V1 local page now exists in workshop/ with starter Melffy, Live Twin, and Traptrix lists.
Good candidate for future shelf/workshop integration.

gremlinProjects.name: Workshop Project Board
gremlinProjects.description: Set up part of the workshop to read this JSON file and display the projects on a little board with names and descriptions.
gremlinProjects.nextStep: Build the first simple reader/view that renders the project list from projects.json.
notes: This is the immediate first project.
The board should become the pickup surface for future heartbeat gremlin work.

gremlinProjects.name: Steam Backlog + Sale Triage
gremlinProjects.description: Turn Nats's Steam library and sale blobs into a useful triage helper so Nyats can separate owned-but-unplayed backlog nudges from true missing-game recommendations.
gremlinProjects.nextStep: Add a tiny workflow or workshop toy that ingests pasted Steam library table HTML and produces owned filters, backlog nudges, and missing-game recommendation lanes.
notes: Prompted after Nyats recommended several games Nats already owns.
Should distinguish 'buy this' from 'you already own this, play it' automatically.
Could live as either a small workshop parser toy or a lightweight helper script plus notes.

gremlinProjects.name: Character Theme Generator
gremlinProjects.description: A rerollable design helper that mixes creature DNA, folklore notes, story briefings, and reference image pulls into character concept prompts.
gremlinProjects.nextStep: Improve the folklore/reference library and add richer save/export options for favorite rolls.

versionNotes.summary: Seed roster expanded with Moth oracle (moth/lunar/omen/nocturnal) and Tidal drift (sea/tide/salt/drowning) entries. Both auto-populate in the dropdown via Object.entries().

notes: Requested by Nats for creature-coded design brainstorming when there is not enough spare research time.
Should support rerolling, storing liked generations, and pulling practical reference images.
Seeds now include: floral ghost, candle spirit, drifting familiar, shrine creature, poisonous elegance, moth oracle, tidal drift.

gremlinProjects.name: Intiface Gremlin Bridge
gremlinProjects.description: A private local-control bridge that receives agent-safe network commands and relays them to Intiface Central / Buttplug on Nats's machine.
gremlinProjects.nextStep: Design the private bridge protocol, auth/safety gates, allowed-device model, and a tiny local daemon shape before implementation.
notes: This is explicitly an ours-only private project, not a public workshop/GitHub Pages toy.
Target shape: local bridge service on Nats's machine, explicit device allowlist, strength/duration caps, kill switch, and simple network command schema.
Long-term it could become a proper OpenClaw tool/plugin, but the first version should be a standalone bridge.
